verses.text: When the land is divided, a holy portion is set apart for the LORD, with the sanctuary for the priests. First to God. The best of the land is reserved for His worship.
When the land is divided, a holy portion is set apart for the LORD, with the sanctuary for the priests. First to God. The best of the land is reserved for His worship.
When the land is divided, a holy portion is set apart for the LORD, with the sanctuary for the priests. First to God. The best of the land is reserved for His worship.
When the land is divided, a holy portion is set apart for the LORD, with the sanctuary for the priests. First to God. The best of the land is reserved for His worship.
A portion is given to the Levites and a possession to the city, beside the holy oblation. Provision ordered. God appoints place for His servants and His people alike.
A portion is given to the Levites and a possession to the city, beside the holy oblation. Provision ordered. God appoints place for His servants and His people alike.
The prince has his portion on both sides, that 'my princes shall no more oppress my people.' Bounded power. Even rulers are kept within limits, lest they wrong the people.
The prince has his portion on both sides, that 'my princes shall no more oppress my people.' Bounded power. Even rulers are kept within limits, lest they wrong the people.
God charges the princes, 'let it suffice you,' to remove violence, do justice, and keep 'just balances.' Honest rule. Leaders are to govern with equity, not greed.
God charges the princes, 'let it suffice you,' to remove violence, do justice, and keep 'just balances.' Honest rule. Leaders are to govern with equity, not greed.
The measures of ephah, bath, and shekel are fixed to one standard. Fair dealing. God cares that weights and measures be honest among His people.
The measures of ephah, bath, and shekel are fixed to one standard. Fair dealing. God cares that weights and measures be honest among His people.
The appointed oblation of wheat, oil, and a lamb is for the offerings, 'to make reconciliation for them.' Atonement provided. Worship centres on the offerings that reconcile to God.
The appointed oblation of wheat, oil, and a lamb is for the offerings, 'to make reconciliation for them.' Atonement provided. Worship centres on the offerings that reconcile to God.
The appointed oblation of wheat, oil, and a lamb is for the offerings, 'to make reconciliation for them.' Atonement provided. Worship centres on the offerings that reconcile to God.
All the people give to the prince, who prepares the offerings 'to make reconciliation for the house of Israel.' Shared worship. The prince leads the nation in approaching God.
All the people give to the prince, who prepares the offerings 'to make reconciliation for the house of Israel.' Shared worship. The prince leads the nation in approaching God.
In the first month the sanctuary is cleansed with a bullock, also for him 'that erreth.' Yearly purging. God provides for the cleansing even of unwitting sin.
In the first month the sanctuary is cleansed with a bullock, also for him 'that erreth.' Yearly purging. God provides for the cleansing even of unwitting sin.
In the first month the sanctuary is cleansed with a bullock, also for him 'that erreth.' Yearly purging. God provides for the cleansing even of unwitting sin.
On the fourteenth day they keep the passover, a feast of seven days with unleavened bread. The feast restored. Redemption is remembered in the renewed worship.
On the fourteenth day they keep the passover, a feast of seven days with unleavened bread. The feast restored. Redemption is remembered in the renewed worship.
The prince prepares the offerings through the feast, and likewise in the seventh month at tabernacles. Faithful keeping. The appointed feasts are kept in their seasons before God.
The prince prepares the offerings through the feast, and likewise in the seventh month at tabernacles. Faithful keeping. The appointed feasts are kept in their seasons before God.
The prince prepares the offerings through the feast, and likewise in the seventh month at tabernacles. Faithful keeping. The appointed feasts are kept in their seasons before God.
